metadata.notes: agy REPL buffers mid-turn input; the filtered transcript (DONE-only) writes no turn boundary for queued text, so the parser sees one contiguous working span. Recipe needs keys + wait_turn — both unelicited → gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** agy runs an interactive bubbletea REPL (binary strings reference `executor has not processed the previous input yet` and `Shell command queued as pending`), so typed input arriving mid-turn is buffered by the runtime rather than starting a new turn — the same queueing model claudecode and codex have.

**daemon=full (provisional).** The scenario asserts the visible contract: state stays `working` through the queued-message arrival (no `→ ready` flicker, no `→ waiting`), and the cycle still ends at `ready`. The antigravity parser drives `working` only from `USER_INPUT` (turn open) and back to `ready` only from an empty terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE`. The FILTERED transcript.jsonl contains only `status:"DONE"` steps — there is no intermediate/queued status — so a buffered follow-up does NOT appear as a synthetic `USER_INPUT` line until it is consumed. While the first turn runs, the parser sees a contiguous run of `PLANNER_RESPONSE`/tool-result lines = one unbroken working span, exactly the no-flicker invariant. The cycle ends `ready` at the final empty `PLANNER_RESPONSE`. CAVEAT (emission, unconfirmed): no sample exercises mid-turn queueing, so whether agy writes the queued prompt as a mid-turn `USER_INPUT` line BEFORE consumption (which would flicker the span) is unverified — `record` must confirm the single contiguous working span.

**driver=gap:wait_turn.** The recipe needs `keys` (type the follow-up into the TUI mid-turn) and `wait_turn` (block on each turn's completion). Both are `not_implemented` in the scaffolded driver. `record` ports the agy turn-done poll and the raw-keys arm before driving.
caveats: Emission UNCONFIRMED: no sample exercises mid-turn queueing. The filtered transcript is DONE-only, which strongly implies a queued prompt is invisible until consumed — but record must verify a single contiguous working span (no →ready flicker when the follow-up lands).
Driver needs keys + wait_turn ported; the recorded gap primitive is wait_turn.
Tokens/cost not in JSONL — observations assert cost/tokens = 0.

sources.note: strings: 'executor has not processed the previous input yet', 'Shell command queued as pending' — runtime buffers input rather than starting a turn
working only from USER_INPUT, ready only from empty terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE; no per-step status drives state
39-line transcript: all steps status DONE; no in-progress/queued status in the filtered view

preconditions: agy CLI >=1.0.10 on PATH, authenticated via Google OAuth
setup: Fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; agy mints its own <conv-id>
First prompt must keep agy busy long enough to type a follow-up (a slow count-to-10)

script.text: Count from 1 to 10, one number per line, pausing briefly between each. On the last line reply with exactly the word: done

script.keys: f o l l o w u p Space q u e u e d Enter

verify: state stays working across the queued-input arrival — no →ready or →waiting flicker
the cycle eventually ends at ready
